reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: Grounded in code: models/bge-sentence-similarity/worker.js loads the REAL transformers.js feature-extraction (declared as sentence-similarity in the catalogue) pipeline (Xenova/bge-small-en-v1.5, WASM, q8) through the shared loadPipeline() and runs pipe(texts,{pooling:'cls',normalize:false}) — CLS pooling (the transformer's first [CLS] token, NOT a mean), then L2-normalises in JS so cosine is a dot product — a genuine on-device forward pass, never a canned result. The immutable conformance suite tests this seam: calls-real-inference (source must match pipeline(|createModelLoader|extractor|classifier|reranker — pass) and loads-clean (console-clean, no uncaught errors on load — pass). The pooling choice is load-bearing and correct: BGE v1.5 is trained with CLS pooling, unlike MiniLM/GTE which mean-pool. The page (not the worker) decides whether to prepend BGE's retrieval query instruction.
rubric.notes: Retrospective backfill: mechanism verified from source + the existing conformance suite; no fresh latency/output numbers were re-measured in this pass (none fabricated).

rubric.evidence: Expected output = one fixed-length 384-d sentence vector per input (CLS-pooled). The page renders a cosine similarity matrix and lets you inspect a vector (real pre-norm magnitude). Conformance io-shape-semantic is manual-evidenced.

At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/bge-small-en-v1.5, ONNX of BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5), the real pipeline task (feature-extraction (declared as sentence-similarity in the catalogue)), backend (WASM — no WebGPU required), quantisation (q8), download size (~34 MB) and license (MIT). Conformance declares-model-id / declares-task / declares-backend / declares-quantisation all pass against these strings.
rubric.notes: At-a-glance discloses CLS pooling + the optional BGE query instruction prefix — the honest BGE recipe, not a generic mean-pool description.

rubric.evidence: The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'Xenova/bge-small-en-v1.5', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:34, requiresWebGPU:false}); a valid current on-device cache auto-initialises with no Load click, and only Download/Re-download/Update surface otherwise. Conformance shared-auto-init-loader (source createModelLoader) passes; auto-init-on-cached is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on Xenova/bge-small-en-v1.5 so the ~34 MB cache is tracked for the never-silently-re-download guarantee.

rubric.evidence: Loading/running/done state is announced in a role=status aria-live=polite region; failures surface ON THE PAGE as an .err status ('Embedding failed: …'), not only the console; the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path. Runs on the WASM path everywhere, so runs-without-webgpu-fallback holds (no GPU gate). Conformance errors-on-the-page + live-status-region pass; honest-progress-retry-offline is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: Scored 4 (not 5): progress/retry/offline are honestly wired but were not re-exercised offline in this retrospective backfill.

rubric.evidence: Real keyboard-operable controls: the sentences textarea, the run button, the inspect-vector <select>, and (per the BGE recipe) the query-instruction toggle. No dead controls.
rubric.notes: Scored 4: controls read as real and keyboard-operable from source, but tap-target sizing has an open automated flag (see accessibility).

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the matrix and inspected vectors are derived from the real CLS-pooled vectors returned by the worker. Conformance no-fake-output is manual-evidenced.

Every button exposes an accessible name (conformance buttons-have-names pass) and status uses role=status aria-live. The similarity matrix sits in an aria-live region. HOWEVER models.json records an automated pre-scan flag for this demo: desktop 13 + mobile 5 sub-24px target(s) — sub-24px tap targets an agent still has to verify, and support is 'needs-review' on both classes. Honest weakness, not yet cleared.
rubric.notes: Light+dark use the WCAG-AA shared design system. The sub-24px tap-target flag is the reason this is 4/minor, not 5.

rubric.evidence: The immutable suite carries responsive-desktop and responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) assertions and the shared design system collapses panels to one column on narrow viewports. BUT support.desktop and support.mobile are both 'needs-review' (not yet confirmed 'ok' by an agent matrix pass) and the automated scan flagged desktop 13 + mobile 5 sub-24px target(s).
rubric.notes: Scored 4: parity is plausibly fine but UNVERIFIED in this backfill; parity-both-classes remains manual-evidenced/awaiting an agent verdict.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated Web Worker; the page renders only bounded reductions (an N×N cosine matrix over a handful of lines) — no main-thread inference and no unbounded per-token loop. Conformance inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced; inference-off-main-thread (source) passes.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — the model forward pass never blocks the interface.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move heavy work to web worker; break up long tasks so the UI stays responsive
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep interactions responsive (INP): move heavy work off the main thread and avoid long blocking tasks.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — every inference call runs in a dedicated module Web Worker (worker.js) via loadPipeline(); the page only posts messages and renders results, so tokenisation + the model forward pass never block the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread pipeline; conformance inference-off-main-thread (source new Worker|worker.js) pass + inp-stays-low

guidanceConsulted.query: break up long synchronous loops and DOM updates to keep the main thread free
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break heavy synchronous processing / DOM updates so the browser can handle input and repaint.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the heavy work (model forward pass) is already off-thread in the worker; the on-page reductions (similarity matrix / projection / score rendering) are bounded over a small number of items, not unbounded per-token loops on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: bounded on-main-thread rendering; worker owns the model pass

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive layout flexible grid no horizontal overflow intrinsic sizing for a control panel and results
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the shared design system (public/styles.css) lays the panels out with flexible grids that collapse to one column on narrow viewports; results scroll inside their own overflow-x container rather than pushing the page wide.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance responsive-desktop + responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) are in the immutable suite

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status announce loading ready error with role=status aria-live; every control has an accessible name
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; use semantic HTML.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the run status uses role=status aria-live=polite, the shared model-loader announces checking/initialising/ready/error, and every button carries a text label.

openQuestions: support.desktop/mobile are 'needs-review' — confirm tap targets and flip support to ok.
Does prepending the BGE query instruction visibly change the matrix on the default corpus, and is that difference explained clearly enough on the page?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass to verify tap targets and clear the sub-24px flag.

summary: Retrospective, code-grounded critique for bge-sentence-similarity (Xenova/bge-small-en-v1.5, feature-extraction / sentence-similarity, WASM q8, ~34 MB, MIT). Real off-thread inference using the CORRECT BGE recipe — CLS pooling (not mean) + optional query instruction — verified from source + the immutable conformance suite; cosine-matrix UI with vector inspection. Open weakness: support 'needs-review' + sub-24px tap-target flag (accessibility/responsive 4/minor). No fabricated numbers.
